seoTitle: Add Custom HTML to Squarespace Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to add custom HTML to your Squarespace site to unlock features like forms, scripts, and custom design elements beyond the defaults.
intro: Squarespace's built-in tools cover a lot, but sometimes you need a custom element — a form, script, or embedded feature — that only custom HTML can provide. This guide explains how to add HTML to Squarespace, from accessing the code editor to troubleshooting issues. It's aimed at website owners and marketers who want more control over their site's functionality without full custom development.

steps.heading: Access the HTML editor
steps.body: Locate the code block or HTML injection option within your Squarespace page editor.

steps.heading: Add your HTML code
steps.body: Paste or write the custom HTML you need directly into the code block on your page.

steps.heading: Preview the changes
steps.body: Check how the added HTML displays on your live page to confirm it renders correctly across devices.

steps.heading: Explore common use cases
steps.body: Custom HTML is often used for embedding forms, third-party widgets, or unique design elements not native to Squarespace.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot HTML issues
steps.body: If something doesn't display correctly, check for syntax errors or conflicts with your site's existing theme styling.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to add HTML to Squarespace?
faq.answer: Basic familiarity with HTML helps, but simple embeds and scripts can often be added by copying and pasting existing code.

faq.question: Can adding custom HTML break my Squarespace site?
faq.answer: Poorly formatted code can cause display issues, so it's best to preview changes before publishing them live.

faq.question: What are common reasons to add HTML to a Squarespace site?
faq.answer: Common uses include embedding forms, adding tracking scripts, or including custom interactive elements.

faq.question: Is custom HTML available on all Squarespace plans?
faq.answer: Code injection features are generally available on most Squarespace plans, though it's worth confirming based on your specific plan.
